blocks.body: Present Continuous: Negatives — am not, isn't, aren't, and verb-ing in negative sentences.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Present Continuous negatives, we put not after the form of be and keep verb-ing.","rules":[{"label":"I am not","color":"emerald","text":"With I, use am not or I'm not.","example":"I am not sleeping. / I'm not sleeping."},{"label":"He / She / It isn't","color":"blue","text":"With he / she / it, isn't is very common.","example":"She isn't working now."},{"label":"You / We / They aren't","color":"amber","text":"With you / we / they, aren't is very common.","example":"They aren't waiting outside."}]}

{"title":"1. Negative formula","intro":"Remember the three patterns with not.","items":[{"label":"I","title":"am not + verb-ing","subtitle":"I am not reading now. / I'm not reading now."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"is not + verb-ing","subtitle":"He isn't sleeping. / It isn't raining."},{"label":"You / We / They","title":"are not + verb-ing","subtitle":"We aren't working today. / They are not coming."},{"label":"Short forms","title":"isn't / aren't / I'm not","subtitle":"In everyday speech, short forms sound natural."}]}

{"title":"2. How the negative works","sections":[{"label":"Not now","color":"emerald","description":"Say what someone is not doing at this moment.","examples":[{"en":"I am not watching TV now.","ua":"I am not watching TV now."},{"en":"They aren't eating at the moment.","ua":"They aren't eating at the moment."}]},{"label":"Not this period","color":"blue","description":"Show that something is not happening in the current period.","examples":[{"en":"She isn't working this week.","ua":"She isn't working this week."},{"en":"We aren't staying at the hotel today.","ua":"We aren't staying at the hotel today."}]},{"label":"Positive vs negative","color":"amber","description":"In the negative, the form of be changes, but verb-ing stays the same.","examples":[{"en":"He is sleeping. → He isn't sleeping.","ua":"is → isn't, but sleeping stays the same."},{"en":"They are waiting. → They aren't waiting.","ua":"are → aren't, but waiting stays the same."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Positive and negative","intro":"In the negative, add not after be.","rows":[{"en":"I am reading now.","ua":"I am not reading now.","note":"am + not + verb-ing"},{"en":"She is working today.","ua":"She isn't working today.","note":"isn't + verb-ing"},{"en":"We are waiting outside.","ua":"We aren't waiting outside.","note":"aren't + verb-ing"}],"warning":"Do not say He doesn't working or She isn't work."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding do / does to Present Continuous.","wrong":"❌ He doesn't working now.","right":"✅ He isn't working now."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Forgetting -ing after be.","wrong":"❌ She isn't work today.","right":"✅ She isn't working today."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Putting not in the wrong place.","wrong":"❌ They not are waiting for us.","right":"✅ They aren't waiting for us."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Present Continuous negative = am / is / are + not + verb-ing.","Use am not or I'm not with I.","With he / she / it, isn't is common.","With you / we / they, aren't is common.","Do not add do / does to this tense."]}
